items.d: after that, when the first thing is done
items.s: We are going to the concert first, and ___ we can all go for a pizza.
items.hintDe: Im Anschluss, kurz nachdem etwas anderes passiert ist.

items.d: not counting one thing, the rest is all true
items.s: ___ his bad taste in music, my dad is really cool and fun to be with.
items.hintDe: Eine Sache ausgenommen, sonst stimmt alles.

items.d: a short show where people watch you and tell you if you are good at music
items.s: She played a pop tune on the flute at her ___ for the school show, and the critics liked it.
items.hintDe: Da zeigst du kurz, was du kannst, damit man dich auswählt.

items.d: not scared, ready for anything scary
items.s: You're a ___ man to get up and play music in front of the whole school — well done!
items.hintDe: Du hast keine Angst und traust dich was.

items.d: somebody whose job is to tell people if music or shows are good or bad
items.s: The ___ on the music show did not like the young singer's voice at all, and everybody could tell.
items.hintDe: Diese Person sagt öffentlich, ob etwas gut oder schlecht ist.

items.d: very, very much
items.s: This famous singer was ___ talented — he played all the music on his record alone.
items.hintDe: Steigerung von sehr: ganz, ganz besonders stark.

items.d: a long, thin musical thing that you play with your mouth and your fingers
items.s: She plays the ___ in the school music club, and her favourite tune is beautiful on it.
items.hintDe: Ein langes, dünnes Blasinstrument zum Spielen von Melodien.

items.d: a way to tell people you really do not like a thing at all
items.s: The noise from the room next to mine is so bad — ___ stand it!
items.hintDe: So sagst du, dass du etwas überhaupt nicht magst.

items.d: a way to tell people a thing is OK for you, you are happy with it
items.s: We can listen to your music if you want — ___ mind, it's all good with me.
items.hintDe: So sagst du, dass dir etwas recht ist und du nichts dagegen hast.

items.d: words you use to show what you think
items.s: ___, this is the best record they have ever made — I love every tune on it.
items.hintDe: So leitest du ein, was du persönlich denkst.

items.d: the words that a singer sings in a piece of music
items.s: I love this tune, but I can never remember the ___ when I want to sing along.
items.hintDe: Das sind die Wörter, die in einem Lied gesungen werden.

items.d: a way to agree when a friend does not like a thing and you do not like it too
items.s: 'I can't listen to their new music any more.' — '___ It's not so good now.'
items.hintDe: So stimmst du zu, wenn jemand sagt, dass er etwas nicht mag.

items.d: used to show a thing is surprising because it is less than you would think
items.s: You're not going to be our new singer — you're ___ going to be at the back of the show.
items.hintDe: Du betonst, dass sogar das Kleinste nicht passiert.

items.d: a big, black, round music thing that you put on a player to play music
items.s: My mum has a box of old black ___ that she still plays on a player on calm days.
items.hintDe: Eine große, runde schwarze Scheibe, die auf einem Plattenspieler läuft.

items.d: somebody who makes music with their voice, and this is their job
items.s: My favourite ___ has a new record out this week, and her voice is amazing on every tune.
items.hintDe: Diese Person macht mit ihrer Stimme Musik.

items.d: having done very well and got what you wanted
items.s: The tune was so ___ that you could listen to it on the radio every day for a whole year.
items.hintDe: Etwas läuft super und kommt gut an.

items.d: a matching jacket and trousers that you wear for special days
items.s: Play your music at home when you're not wearing that smart grey ___ with the matching jacket and trousers.
items.hintDe: Sakko und Hose, die zusammenpassen, für festliche Anlässe.

items.d: very good at a thing, with no help and no work
items.s: My young sister is so ___ at music — nobody taught her, but she plays the flute beautifully.
items.hintDe: Jemand kann etwas von Natur aus richtig gut.

items.d: to think like a friend, with the very opinion they have
items.s: I ___ with you — this singer makes the best music in the whole town!
items.hintDe: Du bist genau derselben Meinung wie jemand anderer.

items.d: to want to find out more about a thing because you like it
items.s: He is really ___ in music — he reads every book about famous singers he can find.
items.hintDe: Du willst mehr über etwas wissen, weil es dich anzieht.

items.w: to be on the way up
items.g: auf dem Weg nach oben sein
items.d: to become more and more famous, doing very well
items.s: That young drummer is really on the ___ up — every month she plays bigger and bigger concerts.

deToEn.text: to be on the way up

enToDe.text: auf dem Weg nach oben sein

items.hintDe: Mit dir geht es bergauf, du wirst immer erfolgreicher.

items.d: when a thing is yours and not a friend's
items.s: This old record isn't mine — it ___ to my mum, so I have to give it back to her.
items.hintDe: Etwas ist deins oder gehört jemandem.

items.d: to do a happy thing because of a good day, like a birthday
items.s: To ___ music, the radio played all our favourite tunes the whole day long.
items.hintDe: Du machst etwas Schönes wegen eines besonderen Tages.

items.d: to go somewhere with a friend
items.s: We're all going to the concert tonight. Do you want to ___ along with us?
items.hintDe: Du gehst mit jemandem zusammen irgendwohin.

items.d: to be happy, sad, or scared inside you
items.s: Whenever I ___ sad, I listen to my favourite tune and it makes me happy again.
items.hintDe: Etwas geht in dir vor, du hast ein Gefühl.

items.d: to talk to a friend again after, not now
items.s: I have no time at the music club now, but I get ___ to you with a yes or no after lunch.
items.hintDe: Du meldest dich später wieder bei jemandem.

items.d: to not like a thing any more because you have done it too much
items.s: I love this music so much that I never ___ tired of listening to it.
items.hintDe: Du magst etwas nicht mehr, weil es dir zu viel wurde.

items.d: to do a thing once to find out if you like it or can do it
items.s: I have never played the flute, but I want to ___ it a try at the music club this week.
items.hintDe: Du probierst etwas einfach einmal aus.

items.d: to not keep doing a thing, because you do not want to do it any more
items.s: Playing the guitar is not always fun at first, but please don't ___. Keep playing every day and you are going to play well.
items.hintDe: Du hörst auf und versuchst es nicht weiter.

items.w: to have got what it takes
items.d: to be good, with all you need to do very well
items.s: You are a great dancer and so musical — you've got ___ it takes to be a famous singer!

deToEn.text: to have got what it takes

items.hintDe: Du hast genau das, was man braucht, um es zu schaffen.

items.d: to do very well and become famous, or to be there at a place
items.s: Sorry, I can't ___ it to your concert tonight — I have to look after my young sister.
items.hintDe: Du schaffst es — oder du schaffst es, dabei zu sein.

items.d: to think of a new thing that is not real, just in your mind
items.s: My young sister likes to ___ up funny dances for her favourite tunes before bedtime.
items.hintDe: Du denkst dir etwas Neues aus, das es so nicht gibt.

items.d: to look like a thing is true, from how it looks to you
items.s: That singer ___ to play worse every time she becomes more famous, in my opinion.
items.hintDe: Etwas wirkt so, sieht für dich nach etwas aus.

items.d: to join in with your voice when music is playing
items.s: Everybody in the car began to ___ along to their favourite tune on the radio.
items.hintDe: Du singst mit, wenn Musik läuft.

items.d: to let a drink fall out of its glass without wanting to
items.s: Be careful with that glass of milk near the record player, or you are going to ___ it all over the table!
items.hintDe: Aus Versehen kippt dir ein Getränk um.

items.d: to happen at a certain time, like a concert or a show.
items.s: Where did the concert ___ place — at the school or in the city park?
items.hintDe: Etwas passiert zu einer bestimmten Zeit an einem bestimmten Ort.

items.d: to use too much of a thing, or use it in a bad way for no good reason
items.s: Don't ___ our time — you are never going to be a famous singer, so please leave now.
items.hintDe: Du gehst mit etwas leichtfertig um und gebrauchst zu viel.

items.d: a piece of music that you can sing along to
items.s: I like music with a good ___ that you can sing along to in the car.
items.hintDe: Die Melodie eines Liedes, die man leicht mitsingen kann.

items.d: not happy, feeling sad inside
items.s: Don't be too ___ that the critics did not like it — you did your best, and that is all good.
items.hintDe: Das Gegenteil von glücklich, du bist traurig.

items.d: all of a thing, with nothing missing
items.s: For 600 euros, your driver is going to drive you behind your favourite singer for the ___ day.
items.hintDe: Das Ganze, alles davon, nichts fehlt.
